steps.title: Join two complete clauses
steps.content: The sentence has two complete clauses: everyone conceded that North America's geographic center was in North Dakota, and establishing the exact coordinates proved more divisive. The blank must connect those independent clauses correctly.

steps.title: Choose the clause punctuation
steps.content: Choice A uses a spaced colon awkwardly before a contrasting independent clause. Choice B creates a comma splice. Choice C creates a run-on. The remaining choice uses a semicolon to join the two complete, closely related clauses. The answer is D.
